title: A prenatal skin atlas reveals immune regulation of human skin morphogenesis
authors: Gopee N, Winheim E, Olabi B, et al.
summary: Comprehensive multi-omics atlas of human prenatal skin (7-17 PCW). Early LE is a fine-grained annotation under the broad Lymphatic endothelium category representing an early/progenitor lymphatic endothelial cell state. LE is defined as 'lymphatic endothelium' in the figure legend. Endothelial cells including lymphatic endothelium are present throughout gestation. Co-location with macrophages in neurovascular microenvironments is predicted.
quotes: Predicted ligand-receptor interactions were consistent with reciprocal communication between macrophages and endothelial cells to support angiogenesis, chemotaxis and cell migration (for example, CXCL8-ACKR1 and CCL8-ACKR1)
Visium deconvolution analysis predicted co-location of prenatal skin macrophages with endothelial cells ('early and late neurovascular microenvironments', ME1 and ME5)
Mesoderm-derived cells, including skin fibroblasts and endothelial cells, and immune cells were present throughout gestation
immune cells are required to fully recapitulate in vivo endothelial cell development
we observed fewer and less heterogeneous endothelial cells in the immunodeficient SkOs compared to prenatal skin, despite the formation of well-developed HFs, epidermis and neural cells
